name: Super Bowl Sunday Stew
recipeIngredient: 1 Pound Beef Stew, cut into 1-inch cubes
2 Carrots, peeled (cut in rounds or quarters)
1 Package Frozen Okra, cut into 10 ounces
16 Ounces Canned Corn OR 10 Ounces Frozen Corn
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 4½ quart stew pot, sauté the beef pieces in butter over medium-high heat until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
2. Add the remaining ingredients and the herb sachet one by one. Note: The block of frozen okra can be added as is; it will quickly thaw in the warming liquid.
3. As the stew cooks throughout the day, add water to achieve your desired thickness (about 4 cups for a thick stew).
4. Bring the stew to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer, partially covered, for at least 6 hours.
5. Stir the stew frequently during cooking.
6. Near the end of cooking, taste for seasoning. Add a pinch of cayenne for extra heat if desired; avoid overloading with spice.
7. Serve hot from the stew pot into heated bowls. A coarse French peasant bread served in large chunks and with plenty of butter complements this dish well.
8. Note: This recipe is ideal for a crockpot, but at least a 5-quart (I use a 6-quart) size is recommended.
